relations.note: Footnote symbol assignment mechanics depend on the 'footnote symbols consumed' metadata field declared per section.
Both are assembler-enforced adjacency checks keyed on declared section metadata.

quotes: self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding
Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; **no margins outside the section** — assembly is direct stacking.

rules.intent: Guarantee predictable, collision-free stacking of modular email sections across templates.
rules.rule_text: Each email section is a self-contained table-based block at the canvas width (600px) with its own top/bottom 32px padding and a declared background color; sections stack directly one after another with no margins outside the section boundary.

quotes: Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.

rules.intent: Give the assembler machine-readable facts needed to enforce adjacency and footnote rules automatically.
rules.rule_text: Every section's metadata must declare: its background color, whether it establishes dermatomyositis (DM) context, which footnote symbols it consumes, and whether it contains a CTA — this metadata drives downstream assembly checks (adjacency, footnote numbering, CTA counting).

quotes: Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time** — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.

rules.intent: Prevent footnote symbol collisions when independently authored sections are combined into one email.
rules.rule_text: Footnote markers (superscript symbols such as † and ‡) are assigned in order of appearance only at assembly time, not authored per-section; sections must reference placeholder tokens (e.g., {{fn1}}) rather than hard-coded daggers whenever more than one footnoted claim exists, so numbering never collides across sections.

quotes: Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).

rules.intent: Avoid broken visual continuity when core-team-controlled locked components are swapped independently of campaign sections.
rules.rule_text: Locked core components — header, footer, and ISI (top_matter/end_matter) — are inserted by the core team; any section placed adjacent to them must not carry decorative elements whose visual continuity depends on what sits above or below (e.g., a wave motif intended to run into the footer).

quotes: Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).

rules.intent: Preserve visual separation and contrast rhythm between dark sections in assembled emails.
rules.rule_text: Dark-background sections must never be placed directly next to another dark-background section; the assembler enforces this using each section's declared background-color metadata.

quotes: All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

rules.rule_text: All colors in code must be expressed as hex, all sizing in px, and CSS must be inline only for email-client compatibility; bgcolor attributes must be duplicated alongside CSS background declarations to support Outlook.
